steps.title: Separate the two sentences
steps.content: The first independent clause ends after United States. The words the study examined begin another independent clause, following the introductory phrase led by the researcher Laura A. Burkle.

steps.title: Fix the sentence boundary
steps.content: A creates a comma splice between independent clauses. B uses and without properly connecting the later independent clause. C leaves the clauses fused with no punctuation. The remaining choice ends the first sentence and starts the second with the participial phrase Led by the researcher Laura A. Burkle. The answer is D.
